label: Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation

node.label: Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation
node.definition: Calibration and risk evaluation test whether predicted probabilities, abstention scores, intervals, or confidence estimates match observed correctness and harm.
node.why_it_matters: Calibration and risk evaluation test whether confidence estimates, abstention behavior, and risk scores remain reliable enough for the intended deployment decision.
limitations: A model can appear calibrated on one sample or score bucket while failing under distribution shift, subgroup differences, or changed decision thresholds.

dependencies.rationale: Calibration and risk evaluation measure whether calibration, uncertainty, and risk objectives behave as intended on held-out data.
Calibration and risk evaluation use benchmarks, test suites, and task metrics to define scored evaluation cases.
Calibration and risk evaluation analyze predicted distributions, uncertainty estimates, and belief-like confidence scores.
